intro.title: Part VIII as a field manual
intro.subtitle: Read the round structure first. Then use the phase sections and action procedures. The full Part VIII text stays below for exact wording and edge cases.

foundations.title: Round Structure
foundations.summary: A game lasts up to 5 rounds. Each round resolves in strict order: Movement, Assault, Combat, then Scoring & Cleanup.
bullets: Learn the phase order before learning the exceptions.
Use the phase spine below when you need to answer: what can I do right now?

foundations.title: Activation System
foundations.summary: During Phases 1–3, players alternate activating one eligible unit at a time. The first player marker decides who starts each phase.
bullets: Each activated unit performs exactly one action from that phase.
Passing matters because the first player to pass claims the first player marker for the next phase.

foundations.title: Deployment & Reserves
foundations.summary: Units begin in reserves, then enter the battlefield during the Movement Phase as supply allows.
bullets: Check the supply pool and available supply before deploying.
Deployment restrictions and zone-of-influence limits still apply when entering the battlefield.

phases.title: Phase 1 — Movement
phases.purpose: Deploy, reposition, disengage, and set up the next phase.
notes: The first player to pass takes the first player marker for the Assault Phase.

procedures.title: Hold
procedures.what: Take no action and still count as activated for the phase.
procedures.when: Movement Phase, when the unit is eligible to activate.
how: Do nothing.
Set the unit's activation marker.

procedures.title: Move
procedures.what: An unengaged unit changes position on the battlefield.
procedures.when: Movement Phase, when the unit is unengaged and activated.
how: Choose a leading model and move it up to Speed, measuring the actual path travelled.
Apply standard movement restrictions: other units' bases, terrain size, gap clearance, and elevation changes.
Set the rest of the unit back into coherency.
cannot: Pass through enemy models or friendly models from other units.
End overlapping another base or impassable terrain.
Change elevation without an ACCESS POINT.

procedures.title: Disengage
procedures.what: An engaged unit withdraws from melee.
procedures.when: Movement Phase, when the unit begins engaged.
how: Move using the standard movement procedure.
Each model must finish strictly outside the engagement range of all enemy units.
Models that fail to clear are removed.
cannot: Leave the leading model inside enemy engagement range.
Charge or make a ranged attack next Assault Phase unless the supply exception applies.
procedures.ifFails: If the leading model cannot finish outside engagement range, the disengage fails, the unit does not move, the leading model is removed, and the unit's activation ends.

procedures.title: Deploy
procedures.what: A unit from reserves enters the battlefield.
procedures.when: Movement Phase, if the unit fits within available supply.
how: Confirm the unit's current supply value fits within available supply.
Set the leading model in contact with the entry edge outside the battlefield, then move it up to Speed using standard movement rules.
Set the rest of the unit in coherency.
cannot: Finish in the enemy zone of influence.
Ignore normal movement restrictions after entering.

phases.title: Phase 2 — Assault
phases.purpose: Deal damage with ranged attacks, run for position, or charge into engagement.
notes: The first player to pass takes the first player marker for the Combat Phase.
This is the phase where target legality, batching, and charge legality matter most.

procedures.title: Run
procedures.what: An unengaged unit repositions instead of attacking.
procedures.when: Assault Phase, when the unit is unengaged and activated.
how: Use the same standard movement procedure as a Move.
Nominate a leading model, move it up to Speed, then rebuild the rest in coherency.
cannot: End within engagement range of enemy units.
Ignore normal movement restrictions or gap clearance.

procedures.title: Hold
procedures.what: Take no action in the Assault Phase.
procedures.when: Assault Phase, when the unit is eligible to activate.
how: Do nothing.
Set the activation marker to show the unit has acted in Assault.

procedures.title: Ranged Attack
procedures.what: An activated unit attacks a legal target with eligible assault weapons.
procedures.when: Assault Phase, when the unit has a valid target and weapon profile.
how: Check target eligibility: visibility, range, target tag, and engagement status.
Assign weapons into batches. A batch is the same weapon profile, from eligible models, into the same target.
Resolve each batch through Attack Pool → Armour Pool → Damage Pool.
Apply evade only if the target qualifies for an evade roll.
cannot: Split the same weapon profile across multiple targets.
Let an unengaged attacking unit target an engaged unit except through template spillover.
Ignore batch order or template rules when they apply.

procedures.title: Charge
procedures.what: An unengaged ground unit moves into engagement range of one or more declared ground targets.
procedures.when: Assault Phase, after declaring targets and rolling charge distance.
how: Declare all intended target units first and nominate one target model in each.
Roll 1D6 and add the result to the unit's Speed.
Move the leading model to a legal final position within engagement range of every declared target, then rebuild coherency.
cannot: Charge flying units or charge with a flying unit.
Touch undeclared enemies, overlap bases, or end on impassable terrain.
Ignore the requirement to legally engage every declared target.
procedures.ifFails: If no legal final position exists, the charge fails and the unit does not move.

phases.title: Phase 3 — Combat
phases.purpose: Resolve obligatory melee between engaged ground units.
notes: Flying units are never engaged and never act in this phase.

procedures.title: Close Combat Attack
procedures.what: An engaged ground unit attacks enemy units it is already engaged with.
procedures.when: Combat Phase, when the engaged unit is activated.
how: Optionally Close Ranks up to 3 inches while staying focused on currently engaged enemies.
Set models base-to-base where possible.
Resolve the melee attacks against enemy units the unit is engaged with.
cannot: Bring the unit into engagement with new enemy units during Close Ranks.
Activate unengaged or flying units in this phase.

phases.title: Phase 4 — Scoring & Cleanup
phases.purpose: Determine marker control, score the round, clean the battlefield, and set initiative for the next round.
notes: This phase resolves in a fixed order.
Marker control depends on contesting eligibility and current supply, not just model count.

procedures.title: Determine Mission Marker Control
procedures.what: Work out which units are allowed to contest each mission marker, then compare supply totals.
procedures.when: Start of the Scoring & Cleanup Phase.
how: A unit contests only if it is on the battlefield, within 3 inches, has line of sight to the marker, is on the same elevation, and is in coherency.
Sum the current supply of all contesting units for each player.
Higher total controls the marker.
cannot: Flying units cannot contest or control mission markers.
Out-of-coherency units cannot contest or control mission markers.

procedures.title: Score Victory Points
procedures.what: Both players tally points from the mission card.
how: Apply the mission card's scoring conditions simultaneously.

procedures.title: End of Game Check
procedures.what: Check whether the mission ends the game at this point.
procedures.when: Immediately after victory points are scored.
how: Follow the mission card's end-of-game condition before resolving later cleanup steps.

procedures.title: Cleanup & Refresh
procedures.what: Resolve end-of-round effects, then clear temporary elements and refresh the battlefield state.
procedures.when: After the end-of-game check, before initiative for the next round.
how: Resolve all end-of-round effects in the required order.
Remove tokens and markers except the listed stay-in-play exceptions.
Refresh exhausted cards and clear activation state for the next round.
see: 8.9.4 Resolve End of the Round Effects

procedures.title: Determine Initiative
procedures.what: Assign the first player marker for the next round.
procedures.when: End of the Scoring & Cleanup Phase.
how: The player with fewer victory points takes the first player marker.
If tied, conduct a roll-off.
